title: Outcome-tracking dashboard (published/live, accuracy, completeness)
context: As the project scales past M2, a lightweight dashboard that tracks published-live counts, alignment accuracy, pronunciation error rate, and consent/rights completeness over time is needed to maintain sustainability and catch quality regressions. The dashboard consumes the quality-metrics dataset (data-003) and per-work records. It is a backlog item that becomes useful once meaningful batch data exists.
objective: Implement a lightweight outcome-tracking dashboard (or report generator) that reads the quality-metrics dataset and per-work records to surface published-live counts, alignment accuracy trends, and consent/rights completeness per work/collection and run.
acceptanceCriteria: Reads the quality-metrics dataset (data-003) and per-work records to compute and display: total chapters published and live, acceptance rate per run, median alignment error per run, pronunciation error rate per run, audio-quality conformance rate per run, consent completeness (%), and rights completeness (%).
Outputs a human-readable summary (Markdown or HTML) and a machine-readable JSON summary per run.
Detects and highlights regressions: any metric that deteriorates by more than a configurable threshold vs. the previous run is flagged.
Passes pnpm build and pnpm test; pnpm lint clean.
Licensed MIT; DCO sign-off on all commits.
resources: PLAN.md (success metrics table, outcome-tracking dashboard, sustainability section)
output: A merged PR adding pipeline/dashboard/ with the outcome-tracking dashboard or report generator, unit tests, and documentation covering the input data format, output summary format, and regression-flag thresholds.
